title: Chapter 426 –  Martial Lineage (7)
content:
Hazy moonlight seeped into the silence.

The banquet hall was lined with rows of precious lanterns.

It had fallen quiet the moment the Peng Family Head stepped inside, but with the arrival of Desolate Fortress's Purple Rank, an icy stillness descended. No one spoke.

To Jeong Yeon-shin, it made no difference either way.

The presence of these crowds, supposedly renowned in the righteous martial world, meant less to him than the years he had spent training Shin So-bin.

—How did you do that? What kind of footwork is it?

—As my achievement rises, energy manipulation becomes incredibly fast. What mindset did you have when creating it? Squad Leader, you don't even need the bathroom since you've reached the Greater Heavenly Circulation, right? Huh? Even if you're going on missions, please teach me this! I'm just joking!

Jeong Yeon-shin stood taller than the Peng Family Head. From where he stood, he could clearly see the hair falling beside Shin So-bin's ear as that massive hand gripped her neck.

True to her nature as a martial artist under the aggressive Radiant Demon Wing, she was not trembling — but she would lose her life in an instant if the Peng Family Head so desired.

'Bastard.'

Blue lightning flashed in his eyes. It was the divine light of the Lowly Revelation Technique, surging with fierce anger.

What Jeong Yeon-shin saw at that moment was the back of a middle-aged man in an elaborate yellow robe that reached his ankles. But what actually flickered across his vision was the Peng Family Head's aura — hundreds of layers of colorless concentric circles radiating outward.

Incredibly dense. Unmatched by anyone else in this banquet hall.

This was not an opponent to be taken lightly. A single careless moment might lead to death from a killing strike that pierced through several layers of defense.

Jeong Yeon-shin paid no mind to that fact as he reached for the back of the Peng Family Head's neck.

In that instant, the formula of the Eternal Blossom Fist coursed like lightning through his arm, and his right hand shot forward, racing the moonlight pouring down from above.

Space split for a moment, parting like a swamp.

Swoosh.

The Peng Family Head's nape settled comfortably into his grasp.

It felt like clutching an invisible barrier of water. It had to be the protective energy technique from the Peng Family's secret arts. The sensation of fluctuating energy against his palm was deeply unpleasant.

"...!"

A startled reaction penetrated through his hand.

It was not the Peng Family Head alone. The collective shock of every person filling the banquet hall prickled against his skin.

Sharp intakes of breath shattered the silence, and in that brief moment, everyone poured out words at once, flooding Jeong Yeon-shin's ears.

"Desolate Fortress... Purple Rank?"

"What's happening right now?"

"We must aid the Family Head...!"

"Stop! You have neither the justification nor the standing!"

Jeong Yeon-shin silently ground his foot into the floor.

Boom!

A translucent shockwave rippled outward in a circle. Tables and wine cups rattled violently from the heavy vibration, and dust swirled up in chaotic plumes.

The crowd fell silent. Into that silence, Jeong Yeon-shin slowly parted his lips.

"Noisy."

He was not venting his anger. He was merely reminding them of the dignity of the Purple Rank.

He knew they had done nothing particularly wrong.

How many people in the world could restrain one of the Eight Family Heads? Excluding the leaders of the great sects, the number would be vanishingly small.

Therefore, all that was permitted to these pebbles of the martial world was to watch as Radiant Demon Wing's Shin So-bin — who had sincerely inherited the techniques of Desolate Fortress's Lightning Genius — was subdued by this man called the Peng Family Head.

'Specks of dust.'

Jeong Yeon-shin lowered his gaze slightly.

A blue blade was touching his side. The wave pattern engraved on its surface was all he needed to identify the weapon.

It was the Peng Family Head's exclusive weapon, the Majestic Soaring Divine Blade — infamous throughout the martial world.

The Peng Family Head had drawn it with blinding speed and pressed it against Jeong Yeon-shin the moment he extended his grappling technique.

The posture of holding the blade's handle in reverse without even looking back. It radiated the aura of a master.

"...I know you well."

A thick voice flowed out.

"Blood of the Tyrant Hero... As I've heard, your temperament runs hot."

The Peng Family Head spoke. The night air rippled finely in that instant — the energy waves emanating from his voice.

His mastery of qi-infused speech, which could lend weight to even trivial talk, was profound. It suited the head of a martial clan who often had to address crowds.

But Jeong Yeon-shin's expression remained unchanged. There was no reason to be unsettled by words so far from the truth.

What mattered more was the behavior of the one who still refused to release Shin So-bin's neck.

"Peng Family Head, you seem to mistake this place for the territory of the Peng clan."

Jeong Yeon-shin moved his lips slowly.

"Is this a declaration of war against the main fortress?"

Words that could only be uttered by someone who fully represented Desolate Fortress. Yet now, the robe he wore was no longer black.

"Your words are excessive. This hardly warrants talk of a sect war."

The absolute ruler of the Peng Family spoke. His tone seemed almost coaxing. Jeong Yeon-shin did not even snort.

"Peng Family Head, let go."

"Why don't you withdraw your hand first? Despite your high position, you shouldn't disregard seniority like this. Strictly speaking, you've received a favor from our family."

"Favor?"

"Surely you know that our family contributed to your promotion to Purple Rank."

There was no wavering in his voice. He truly seemed to believe it.

A tone suggesting that where something is given, something must be returned. Here stood the head of one of the Eight Families — a man who was equivalent to royalty within his own territory.

The Peng Family Head moved only his chin while keeping his back to Jeong Yeon-shin.

"Anyway, things have turned unfortunate."

"It would be better not to speak further."

"Though I could have ignored the whistle blowing from Huguang... if not for a variable like you, it certainly would have been so. Truly regrettable."

The Peng Family Head said. The whistle from Huguang. He was referring to the whistle that symbolized the Divine Sword Group Leader, Yong Hye-myeong. It was a provocation.

"You're accumulating bad karma with your mouth."

Jeong Yeon-shin's response was calm. Since the moment he saw this enemy holding Shin So-bin's lifeline, the crown of his head had gone white with fury.

It was the surrounding crowd that kept showing signs of shock at his words. Their revered Eight Family Head was being subjected to outright contempt.

But the Peng Family Head himself only moved his mouth calmly.

"Let's both let go on the count of two. I stake the honor of my family."

It was a proposal that could not easily be refused. Because it was not about Jeong Yeon-shin himself — it was about the life of the top disciple of the Lightning Genius Martial Lineage.

***

Lazy Flame Dragon and Hyeon Won-chang stood about twenty paces from their squad leader.

They were squarely caught in the energy waves radiating from the two powerful figures, yet close enough to leap in if necessary.

"That robe... the color is the same as the Divine Sword Group Leader's?"

"To my eyes as well, it appears purple. Orange, purple, crimson... even the material that shifts color depending on the viewing angle is the same. It's quite mysterious..."

"Hmph."

"Yes, finally...!"

Hyeon Won-chang raised his tightly clenched fist.

Lazy Flame Dragon, who rarely let out even a low exclamation, was silent for a moment. Then he quietly caressed a half-drawn flower tucked against his chest.

The opium poppy is a short-lived plant — two years at most. Jeong Yeon-shin had donned the purple robe just as the flower seed, born with his initiation, was on the verge of withering.

"So it's... possible."

Lazy Flame Dragon murmured.

But for now, that was the limit of the reaction either he or Hyeon Won-chang could show.

They watched with long, slow exhalations as Shin So-bin was freed from the Peng Family Head's hand.

Then they witnessed the Peng Family Head turning to face Jeong Yeon-shin, as if his interest in her had already faded.

Two supreme masters faced each other.

The atmosphere was explosive. The air itself seemed stretched to its breaking point.

The first to speak was the Peng Family Head.

"Let's end it here today. This is nominally a sect sparring conference. We should maintain proper etiquette, shouldn't we? The matchups haven't even been announced yet."

"The first duel will be between Desolate Fortress and the Peng Family. I was on my way back after confirming this."

Jeong Yeon-shin answered in a cold voice.

Lazy Flame Dragon and Hyeon Won-chang sensed an unprecedented rigidity in their squad leader. It was the righteous anger that a head of a family would harbor.

"Ah."

The Peng Family Head smiled.

"Yes, you were the origin of all this. Someone I must definitely kill later. But there is a proper order to all undertakings. Our family is not yet prepared, so as the family head, I request a formal duel at the scheduled time. As for the disrespect shown to Desolate Fortress's martial artist... as the Peng Family Head, I deeply apologize."

He was avoiding immediate conflict.

Many eyes were watching.

With this much formality, it became difficult to force a fight unreasonably. The conduct the Peng Family Head displayed overwhelmed Jeong Yeon-shin's firm resolve.

Meanwhile, the Peng Family Head's eyes ceaselessly scanned Jeong Yeon-shin's entire body. Colorless divine light left faint streaks here and there, tracing his gaze.

"That..."

Lazy Flame Dragon had once been the young master of the Hwangbo Family. This meant he understood the ways of the martial world. He immediately recognized what kind of trick the Peng Family Head was playing.

"He plans to delay the duel as long as possible so he can use the Heavenly Indian Yoga Technique."

"Heavenly Indian Yoga Technique?"

Hyeon Won-chang asked, while casually patting Shin So-bin's shoulder as she returned to them. The three did not bother exchanging words like "Are you okay?" or "I'm fine."

Lazy Flame Dragon folded his arms.

"The Peng Family has changed greatly since the previous family head, the Ghost Cry Hegemon King. Thanks to the Yoga Divine Skill — a type of Bone Shrinking Technique. They can reshape their bodies at will. They've become strange creatures who can freely extend or contract their acupoints, muscles, arms, and legs to match their opponents."

"So that is...?"

"Since he's seen our squad leader up close, he wants to extract every last bit of information before leaving. In the formal duel, he'll appear with a body composition specifically tailored to counter our squad leader's physique. Even I, as a former young master, can't guess what that might look like..."

"So he's trying to buy time and avoid this immediate confrontation?"

Hyeon Won-chang furrowed his brow. In that brief moment, he had grasped the Peng Family Head's intentions.

Using the divine eye technique unique to supreme masters to examine Jeong Yeon-shin's body, then avoiding immediate combat to prepare for a life-and-death duel.

It was the natural behavior of a martial clan that pursued every possible advantage for its lineage.

Lethal intent.

He meant to kill Jeong Yeon-shin, without question.

Not now — but after reconstructing the Yoga Divine Skill with all the resources the Peng Family could muster.

"It's time for a tongue to intervene."

The Great Hero of Desolate Fortress stepped forward. It had been a while since he had loosened his tongue. Ma Gwang-ik, the Great Snow Sword, was skilled in such situations.

At that moment.

"What's your mother's name?"

"What?"

"Is it Peng Ye-ran, the Ghost Cry Hegemon King? The criminal who murdered Obeying Heaven Wing Leader of the main fortress."

Jeong Yeon-shin asked quietly, looking down at the Peng Family Head — a man with a somewhat sharp impression, holding a large blade in reverse with one hand.

For the first time, an expression crossed the Peng Family Head's face.

"Sichuan, Flower Shadow King... Golden Wing Sect... you couldn't possibly be..."

"Her swordsmanship was refined."

Jeong Yeon-shin's words were brief. But their impact struck the venue with tremendous force. The audience, who had been holding their breath, stirred greatly.

Hyeon Won-chang returned to his place with his mouth hanging open. And after taking in the two supreme masters with his eyes, he suddenly recalled the Divine Sword Group Leader and the Namgung Family Head facing each other in a similar way.

The one-match duel that gossipmongers had endlessly discussed after the sparring between Desolate Fortress and the Namgung Family.

"You, bastard."

The Peng Family Head was different from the Namgung Family Head back then.

Perhaps because he had endured repeated insults that the head of a major clan would find nearly unbearable? His face had lost some of its composure.

"Come."

Jeong Yeon-shin said, drawing a sword that looked deeply unfamiliar to Hyeon Won-chang's eyes. A white, elongated sword light flickered in the night air.

And then the bodies of the two supreme masters vanished.

Boom!

Hyeon Won-chang still had not released the seventy percent restriction. He could not properly track the two figures as they scattered into faint afterimages.

—!

The aftermath of the collision arrived before the sound. An indescribable pressure poured down upon them all.

Amidst the sensation of being crushed from every direction, a sandstorm rose like a tsunami and engulfed the audience.

Hyeon Won-chang barely endured by rooting his feet to the ground with the Thousand Catties Weight technique.

Beside him, Shin So-bin was striking the air with the Eternal Blossom Fist. She was neutralizing the energy waves left behind by the supreme masters' strikes.

[Forest Blade Defies Sealing God.]

It was the Peng Family Head's voice. The intention residing in his upper energy center flowed outward through a cloud-like mass of dense energy.

A phenomenon impossible without the Unity of Spirit and Energy. For a moment, the night sky lit up brilliantly, then returned to darkness.

In contrast, Jeong Yeon-shin's voice was not heard.

A single breath seemed to stretch into eternity. It was difficult to breathe.

But soon, Hyeon Won-chang suddenly exhaled a large breath. He also raised the eyebrows that had been forced down by the sandstorm raging like hidden weapons.

Could it truly have been a one-move duel?

At the center of the banquet hall.

Jeong Yeon-shin stood firm, looking down.

In a posture of lowering his tightly held sword to his toes. Blood flowed freely along the sword's edge as if his grip had been completely torn, but he was clearly standing.

His opponent was not.

The head of the Peng clan, with ties to the imperial court. The revered seat among the Eight Family Heads that the entire martial world looked up to.

The left half of his body had been completely blown away. It was impossible to guess what manner of sword strike he had received.

He simply lay there with his eyes wide open. His face showed anger, fighting spirit, and the recognition of defeat. Judging by his twisted mouth, he seemed to have anticipated his own complete destruction at the very end.

"......"

No one dared to stir. And this despite the fact that only those with widespread fame in the martial world had entered the Green Jade Sword Manor.

At this moment, only one person had the right to speak.

"I, Jeong Yeon-shin of the Jeong Family, the initiator of this Mount Hua Agreement life-and-death duel, hereby announce the result."

Jeong Yeon-shin shook the Remaining Thunder sharply downward. Bright red blood dripped from its edge, and faint moonlight settled upon the droplets.

"The Peng Family has been defeated once."

He certified and announced it himself — alone.

The Purple Rank of Desolate Fortress.

The crowd remained silent before the death of the Eight Family Head, witnessed by all.
